description:
# Why

`src/cli.rs` is 305 lines, over the 300-line pre-commit cap. The
line-length gate (`scripts/check-line-lengths.sh`, also in `make
check` and the pre-commit hook) fails on it.

It landed broken: bl-32e5 (`5bac85c`, "bl plugin enable/disable/list")
grew `cli.rs` from 299 -> 305. `bl review`'s squash-merge does not run
the pre-commit hook, so the gate never fired on that delivery. main
has been red on `make check` since.

# Fix

Follow the in-tree decomposition convention: lift a cohesive unit into
a sibling module and re-export it so callers stay byte-stable.
`cli.rs` is already split once — `cli_sub.rs` holds the subcommand and
value enums. Lift the `Command` subcommand enum into a new
`cli_command.rs` the same way, and `pub use` it back from `cli`.

- `cli.rs` keeps only the `Cli` parser struct plus the re-exports.
- `cli_command.rs` holds `pub enum Command`, importing the `cli_sub`
  types its variants reference.
- `main.rs` gains `mod cli_command;`. Its `use cli::{... Command ...}`
  is unchanged — `cli::Command` resolves through the re-export.

Pure code movement: no behavior change, coverage stays 100% by
construction.
title: Decompose src/cli.rs under the 300-line cap
